02/22/2026.example: The tiny moons are embedded within Saturn's rings.

02/22/2026.definition: the gradual collection or increase of material or substance
02/22/2026.example: The buildup of sediment can change the shape of riverbanks.

02/22/2026.definition: confined or restricted to a particular place
02/22/2026.example: The localized flooding affected only a few streets.

02/22/2026.definition: to arrange or perform an event or show
02/22/2026.example: She put on puppet shows dramatizing Puerto Rican folktales.

02/22/2026.definition: in a way that is irregular, inconsistent, or unpredictable
02/22/2026.example: Moths fly erratically around light sources at night.

02/22/2026.definition: not based on reason or logic
02/22/2026.example: People sometimes make irrational economic decisions that defy standard theory.

02/22/2026.definition: something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected
02/22/2026.example: Milkman's research addresses anomalies that standard economic models cannot explain.

02/22/2026.definition: to be the cause, basis, or foundation of something
02/22/2026.example: Cultural frameworks underlie each emotional reaction a person has.

02/22/2026.definition: in a thorough or detailed manner
02/22/2026.example: Hochschild explored the concept at length in her book.

02/22/2026.definition: relating to a system of ideas or beliefs, especially political or cultural
02/22/2026.example: Her study examined the ideological frameworks shaping emotions.

02/22/2026.definition: a basic structure underlying a system, concept, or text
02/22/2026.example: Social and cultural frameworks influence behavior.

02/22/2026.definition: to incorporate beliefs, attitudes, or norms into one's own thinking
02/22/2026.example: People internalize cultural norms through socialization.

02/22/2026.definition: the state of being officially attached to a group or organization
02/22/2026.example: Political affiliation can shape emotional responses.

02/22/2026.definition: a large number or sudden outburst of something, usually negative
02/22/2026.example: After a spate of illnesses as a child, Wilma Rudolph was told she might never walk again.

02/22/2026.definition: to openly resist or refuse to obey
02/22/2026.example: Defying all odds, Rudolph became a champion athlete.

02/22/2026.definition: something unusual or unlikely to happen
02/22/2026.example: She overcame all odds to achieve greatness.

02/22/2026.definition: a short, fast race or run
02/22/2026.example: Rudolph won both the 100- and 200-meter dashes.

02/22/2026.definition: to succeed in achieving or winning something
02/22/2026.example: She clinched first place for her team in the relay.

02/22/2026.definition: a race in which members of a team take turns running parts of the total distance
02/22/2026.example: The 4x100-meter relay is a famous track event.

02/22/2026.definition: based on guesswork or incomplete evidence rather than certainty
02/22/2026.example: The interpretations of Thereus's color are ultimately speculative.

02/22/2026.definition: occurring as a minor or secondary effect, not essential
02/22/2026.example: The gray coloration may be an incidental effect of radiation.

02/22/2026.definition: the physical structure, components, or constitution of something
02/22/2026.example: Scientists study the makeup of outer solar system objects.

03/01/2026.definition: a style of Christian religious music known for strong emotion, powerful vocals, and themes of faith
03/01/2026.example: She began her career as a gospel musician before influencing rock ’n’ roll.

03/01/2026.definition: showing strong feelings, enthusiasm, or deep emotion
03/01/2026.example: Her passionate performances made audiences feel both sorrow and joy.

03/01/2026.definition: made specifically to match the measurements and needs of an individual person
03/01/2026.example: The costume was custom fit to ensure it perfectly suited the actor.

03/01/2026.definition: to start something again after it has stopped, especially a fire or strong emotion
03/01/2026.example: In the film, astronauts attempt to reignite the dying Sun.

03/01/2026.definition: the quality of being clumsy, heavy, or lacking grace in movement or appearance
03/01/2026.example: The bulky suit added to the ungainliness of the character’s movements.

03/01/2026.definition: a small wave or disturbance that spreads outward from a central point
03/01/2026.example: Gravitational waves are ripples in space-time caused by massive cosmic events.

03/01/2026.definition: not yet explained, identified, or included in calculations
03/01/2026.example: Scientists investigated the unaccounted-for oxygen to determine its source.

03/01/2026.definition: a large continuous area of land, such as a continent or large region
03/01/2026.example: Al-Biruni theorized that a vast landmass existed beyond the known boundaries of Europe.

03/01/2026.synonym: period of rapid migration for gold
03/01/2026.definition: a historical period marked by mass migration and economic activity due to the discovery of gold, especially in mid-19th-century California
03/01/2026.example: During the gold rush era, many entrepreneurs built fortunes in California.

03/01/2026.definition: a company or service responsible for transporting passengers or goods
03/01/2026.example: The court ruled that the carrier could not deny passengers service based on race.

03/01/2026.definition: the extent or range of something; width or comprehensiveness
03/01/2026.example: Given the breadth of the collection, the exhibition provided a thorough overview of photography.

03/01/2026.definition: a method of moving the arms and legs in swimming to propel the body through water
03/01/2026.example: The swimmer performed the butterfly stroke with perfect technique.

03/01/2026.definition: to display or wear something with pride
03/01/2026.example: The swimmer sported rainbow-striped goggles.

03/01/2026.definition: having stripes of colors arranged like a rainbow
03/01/2026.example: She wore rainbow-striped goggles in the painting.

03/01/2026.definition: a protective or specialized lens worn over the eyes, often in swimming or science
03/01/2026.example: The swimmer adjusted her goggles before diving in.

03/01/2026.definition: having colors similar to those of a rainbow
03/01/2026.example: The rainbow-hued swimmer leapt gracefully through the water.

03/01/2026.definition: to cause liquid to scatter and fall in drops
03/01/2026.example: The swimmer splashes through the water, creating a vivid effect in the painting.

03/01/2026.definition: having a bill or snout shaped like a duck’s
03/01/2026.example: The duck-billed platypus has a distinctive flat snout for catching prey.

03/01/2026.definition: having a broad, flat tail resembling that of a beaver
03/01/2026.example: Its beaver-tailed body helps it swim efficiently.

03/01/2026.definition: to resist or challenge categorization, expectation, or norm
03/01/2026.example: The platypus defied classification when first discovered.

03/01/2026.definition: a beak or snout resembling that of a bird
03/01/2026.example: Its bird-snout puzzled early scientists.

03/01/2026.definition: the top surface of a building, exposed to sunlight and weather
03/01/2026.example: Dark rooftops absorb heat more than lighter ones.

03/01/2026.definition: manufactured in advance, usually in standard sections, for easy assembly on site
03/01/2026.example: The prefabricated reflective sheets were installed quickly on the roof.

03/01/2026.definition: occurring or existing between two points in time or events
03/01/2026.example: During the intervening 250 years, Donne's works had largely been forgotten.

03/01/2026.definition: to alter the instructions or functioning of a system, device, or molecule
03/01/2026.example: The scientists reprogrammed the genetic scissors to target specific DNA sequences.

03/01/2026.definition: splitting or severing something, often a molecule or chemical bond
03/01/2026.example: DNA-cleaving bacteria produce enzymes that can cut genetic material precisely.

03/01/2026.definition: to form, shape, or create something, often with effort or skill
03/01/2026.example: The researchers used CRISPR to forge a powerful new genetic tool.

03/01/2026.definition: relating to the ability of a material to withstand pulling or stretching forces
03/01/2026.example: Lin Zhi increased the tensile strength of silkworm silk.

03/01/2026.definition: to cause a solid to mix into a liquid and form a solution
03/01/2026.example: The silk was dissolved in a solution of iron, zinc, and sugar.

03/01/2026.definition: to weave again or reorganize fibers into a new structure
03/01/2026.example: Zhi reweaved the silk to enhance its mechanical properties.

03/01/2026.definition: a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances
03/01/2026.example: The silk was treated in a solution of iron ions and sugar.

03/01/2026.definition: to gather information from different sources and organize it into one document or data set
03/01/2026.example: The researchers compiled data from multiple ocean studies conducted over twenty years.

03/01/2026.definition: the speed at which different layers of ocean water blend due to currents and turbulence
03/01/2026.example: A higher ocean mixing rate can distribute heat more evenly across regions.

03/01/2026.definition: new and original; not previously used or discovered
03/01/2026.example: The team created a novel data set by combining measurements from various sources.

03/01/2026.definition: caused or influenced by ocean currents
03/01/2026.example: Current-driven mixing plays a major role in transporting nutrients.

03/01/2026.definition: the way something is spread or arranged over an area
03/01/2026.example: Ocean currents affect the distribution of heat and nutrients worldwide.

03/01/2026.definition: relating to a group of people belonging to the same tribe or indigenous community
03/01/2026.example: The NCAI was founded by representatives of fifty tribal governments.

03/01/2026.definition: the authority of a group or state to govern itself without outside interference
03/01/2026.example: The NCAI was created to protect the sovereignty of Indigenous tribes.

03/01/2026.definition: the act of supporting, promoting, or arguing for a cause or policy
03/01/2026.example: The NCAI's advocacy was key to the passing of Public Law 96-638.
